channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mkapa’s Health Policies Continue to Shape Tanzania’s Healthcare System
item.description: Dr. Nchimbi highlights the enduring impact of former President Benjamin Mkapa's health policies on Tanzania's healthcare landscape. Mkapa, who led Tanzania from 1995 to 2005, implemented reforms that ...

item.title: Lauren Laverne reveals smouldering myeloma diagnosis
item.description: Broadcaster Lauren Laverne has disclosed she has been diagnosed with smouldering myeloma, a blood and bone marrow disorder that can progress to blood cancer. The 48-year-old BBC presenter, who previou...

item.title: Congress Urges Punjab Dissidents to Maintain Discipline and Avoid Public Dissent
item.description: The Congress party has sternly warned dissenting Punjab leaders to adhere to party discipline and avoid airing grievances against the state chief publicly. This came after prolonged public opposition ...

item.title: Five Candidates Compete for Vacant Dasmariñas, Cavite Congressional Seat
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) announced that five candidates have filed certificates of candidacy (COCs) for the special congressional elections in Dasmariñas, Cavite. The vacancy arose after ...

item.title: Second suspect arrested in R14.9m precious stones theft investigation
item.description: The Independent Police Investigative Directorate (IPID) has confirmed the arrest of a second suspect in connection with the alleged theft of R14.9 million worth of precious stones. The suspect, believ...

item.title: Nigerian lawyer withdraws from defending client accused of child defilement after investigation
item.description: Lawyer Francis Paul Chukwuma shared his experience of withdrawing from representing a client accused of defiling a child. The client initially claimed innocence but later admitted to the crime after C...

item.title: India and US Discuss Stricter Visa Rules Impacting Students and Visitors
item.description: India has expressed concerns over the United States' new visa policies that restrict the stay of foreign students, exchange visitors, and journalists. The Trump administration's rules, effective from ...

item.title: Tanzania receives 8 billion USD from Norway for renewable energy projects
item.description: Norway has pledged 8 billion USD to support Tanzania's transition to clean energy, marking a significant step in the country's efforts to combat climate change and promote sustainable development. The...

item.title: China's open-source AI model sparks market volatility
item.description: The release of an open-source artificial intelligence model developed by a Chinese company has led to significant market volatility, with investors reacting to potential implications for global tech c...

item.title: SAPS General Feroz Khan Discharged from Hospital Amid Ongoing Investigation into Shooting
item.description: Maj-Gen Feroz Khan, the suspended deputy national head of SAPS crime intelligence, has been discharged from Milpark Hospital after nearly three weeks following a shooting incident outside his Houghton...

item.title: Sigrid Reflects on Blending Pop and Rock Influences in New Album
item.description: Sigrid, the Norwegian pop artist, discusses her artistic evolution during an interview with NME ahead of her performance at Mad Cool 2026. The article highlights her journey of merging pop sensibiliti...

item.title: Another suspect in Dr. Kent Carpenter's murder case surrenders
item.description: A new suspect in the murder of Dr. Kent Carpenter has surrendered to Philippine authorities, adding to a growing list of individuals linked to the high-profile case. The suspect, identified as part of...

item.title: New Prime Minister Tackles Persistent Economic Growth Challenges in Britain
item.description: The article discusses the challenges faced by Andy Burnham, the new British Prime Minister, as he attempts to address long-standing economic issues. Burnham's plan centers on devolving power to local ...

item.title: Critique of 20th-Century Economic Theories and Their Global Impact
item.description: The article critiques how 20th-century economic theories, such as free trade and market-driven solutions, have failed to address global challenges. It highlights the collapse of the World Trade Organi...

item.title: Niger State Launches Inquiry into Suspected Infectious Disease Following Child's Death
item.description: The Niger State government has initiated an investigation into a suspected infectious disease following the death of a child and reports of other family members falling ill. State Director of Public H...

item.title: Mexico and Canada Continue Trilateral Trade Talks Under CUSMA Agreement
item.description: Mexico's foreign secretary, Roberto Velasco Álvarez, has emphasized that his country is not seeking to negotiate a bilateral trade deal with the U.S. that would exclude Canada, reaffirming the importa...

item.title: Judge Declares Mistrial in Frank Stronach's Sexual Assault Case
item.description: A lawyer for billionaire businessman Frank Stronach has confirmed that a judge has declared a mistrial in his sexual assault conviction, overturning a previous guilty verdict. The ruling came unexpect...

item.title: DJ Josh Fawaz credits AI for 'Like a Prayer' cover
item.description: The article discusses DJ Josh Fawaz's decision to acknowledge artificial intelligence as a collaborator on his cover of the iconic Whitney Houston song 'Like a Prayer'. This move has sparked conversat...

item.title: Survey Reveals 41% of UK Adults Haven't Listened to a Full Album in a Year
item.description: A recent survey conducted by National Rail reveals that 41% of UK adults have not listened to a full album in the past year, highlighting a shift in music consumption habits. The Track Reset campaign,...

item.title: Motorcycle Taxi Riders Push for Safety Measures Like ID Checks and SOS Buttons
item.description: Motorcycle taxi riders in the Philippines are advocating for enhanced safety protocols, including passenger identity verification and an SOS button feature in ride-hailing apps, following a series of ...
